results.rendered_html:
anargha a. [nāsti argho mūlyaṃ yasya na. ba.] Invaluable, priceless, inestimable;  R. 5. 2 ;  Śi. 14. 88. 
— rghaḥ [na. ta.] Wrong or improper value.
Comp.
— rāghavaṃ N. of a drama in 7 acts by Murāri Miśra; also called Murāri Nāṭaka after its author and supposed to have been written between the 12th and the 14th century.
